steps.title: Complete the square for both variables
steps.content:
Starting with $x^2-16x+y^2-10y-55=0$, complete the square for each variable.

For $x$: $x^2 - 16x = (x-8)^2 - 64$
For $y$: $y^2 - 10y = (y-5)^2 - 25$

Substitute back into the original equation:
$$(x-8)^2 - 64 + (y-5)^2 - 25 - 55 = 0$$
$$(x-8)^2 + (y-5)^2 = 144$$

This is the standard form of a circle with center $(8, 5)$ and radius $r = \sqrt{144} = 12$.

steps.title: Use the inscribed circle property
steps.content:
When a circle is inscribed in a square, it touches all four sides of the square. This means the diameter of the circle equals the side length of the square.

The diameter is $2r = 2(12) = 24$, so the square has a side length of 24.

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter of a square with side length 24 is:
$$\text{Perimeter} = 4 \times 24 = 96$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
